failedAssertions.reason: Chat and settings network errors don't show error UI. The session list API unreachable scenario passes, but network errors during chat and settings load fail - no error toast appears when hasErrorUI() is checked.

500 errors during session create and chat API don't show error UI. 500 on profile load and data export DO show error toasts correctly. The issue is specific to session/chat failures - MeditationChat component doesn't properly render error state when beginSession() or sendMessage() fails.

flowReports.notes: Round 5 re-test after add-error-toasts-to-stores. Tests confirm: (1) Toast implementation exists in sessionStore and is called in catch blocks, (2) Toast works for profile load and data export 500s, (3) Toast does NOT work for session create and chat API failures, (4) MeditationChat doesn't properly render error state when status becomes 'error' - UI stays in idle view showing 'Begin Meditation' button.

systemicIssues.description: MeditationChat component doesn't handle error status properly. When sessionStore.beginSession() fails with 500 and sets status='error', MeditationChat falls through to active chat view but the error is not visible because the component renders the idle view (if/else logic issue or state not updating).
systemicIssues.impact: VAL-ERROR-001 (network error during chat) and VAL-ERROR-002 (500 on session create, 500 on chat API) cannot pass - error UI never appears.
systemicIssues.rootCause: Component logic issue in MeditationChat.tsx - the error display div only renders in the 'active chat view' but status='error' may cause incorrect view rendering. Or the toast is added to store but component doesn't re-render.

reRunNotes: Round 5 re-test after add-error-toasts-to-stores (status: completed). Implementation partially works: toast IS added in sessionStore catch blocks, but MeditationChat doesn't render error state properly for session/chat failures. Settings/profile errors work fine. This is a frontend component bug, not a store bug.
